title: Natural Notes — E String
description: Play every natural note on the E string from open to fret 12, naming each note out loud. Then descend back to open. This is the single most important fretboard exercise for a beginner.
instructions: Starting on the open E string (fret 0), play each natural note ascending to fret 12.
Say the note name out loud every time you play: E, F, G, A, B, C, D, E.
At fret 12 (E again), pause, then descend: E, D, C, B, A, G, F, E.
Keep one note per beat — no rushing.
The sharps/flats are the frets you skip. Notice E to F is one fret (half step), and B to C is one fret (half step). All other natural notes are two frets apart (whole step).

variations.label: Harder: play without looking at the tab — recall fret positions from memory
Harder: random natural notes — call out a note name, find it before the next beat

tab.label: Spot drill — pick any natural note and find it

ensembleNotes: The E string is your lowest and most-used string. A bassist who knows the E string cold can find the root of any chord a guitarist calls out. This drill is worth repeating daily until it's automatic.
